Replace blocking alert() with non-blocking console warning in GlobalCard
prs.body:
### Summary

The `copyURLToClipboard()` function in `planet/js/GlobalCard.js` currently uses a blocking 
`alert("Failed to copy!")` when clipboard copy fails. This freezes the UI and interrupts 
user workflow, resulting in poor user experience.

### Changes Made

- Replaced the blocking `alert()` with a non-blocking `console.warn` in the ClipboardJS 
  error callback:
  
  ```js
  console.warn("Failed to copy to clipboard:", e.action);

Issue No. #5483

prs.title: Fix XSS Vulnerability in Toolbar Translations
prs.body:
### Summary
This PR fixes a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Music Blocks toolbar. The issue existed in `js/toolbar.js`, where translation strings were inserted into the DOM using `innerHTML`:

```
elem.innerHTML = obj[1];
```
A malicious translation string could execute arbitrary JavaScript in the user's browser.

### Changes Made
Replaced innerHTML with textContent for translation strings:
```
- elem.innerHTML = obj[1];
+ elem.textContent = obj[1];
```
This ensures all translation text is treated as plain text and cannot execute scripts.

Other uses of innerHTML for icons, images, or templates are left unchanged.

### Testing
Verified toolbar labels display correctly in English and other supported languages.

Confirmed that malicious HTML in translations is displayed as text, preventing XSS.

Tooltips and other UI elements continue to work as expected.

### Security Impact
Prevents XSS attacks through translation files.

Scope limited to toolbar text translations; no impact on other functionality.

### Notes
This is a security-focused patch, no visual/UI changes.

Compatible with all existing toolbar functionality.


Note: This PR addresses a security issue (XSS) in toolbar translations.

prs.title: fix: add root true to prevent ESLint config leakage
prs.body:
Title:-Fix ESLint configuration leakage by setting root to true

Problem

Running npm run lint could fail because ESLint was traversing parent directories and loading unrelated ESLint configuration files from the system (for example, a user-level .eslintrc.js requiring eslint-plugin-react). Since MusicBlocks does not use React or include this plugin, linting would break in such environments.

Solution

This PR adds "root": true to .eslintrc.json, instructing ESLint to stop searching for configuration files beyond the project root. This ensures that only MusicBlocks’ own ESLint configuration is applied.

Result

Prevents ESLint from loading system or parent directory configurations

Avoids unnecessary plugin dependency errors

Makes linting behavior consistent across different developer environments

🔧 Change Summary

Added "root": true to .eslintrc.json

prs.title: Fix/xss faqitem sanitize html
prs.body: --- name: Pull Request about: Submit changes to the Sugar Labs website for review --- ## 📝 Description This PR fixes a potential Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the `FAQItem` component. The component previously used `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` to render FAQ answers without sanitization. Although the current FAQ data is static, this pattern is inherently unsafe and could introduce XSS vulnerabilities if the data source changes in the future (e.g., CMS, API, or external content). To address this, HTML content is now sanitized using **DOMPurify** before being rendered, following security best practices while preserving existing functionality. --- ## 🔗 Related Issue Fixes # (no existing issue – proactive security hardening) --- ## 🔄 Type of Change - [ ] 📱 New Feature (new page, component, or functionality) - [ ] 🎨 UI/UX Update (visual changes, styling improvements) - [ ] 📖 Content Update (text changes, documentation) - [x] 🐛 Bug Fix - [ ] ⚡ Performance Improvement - [ ] ♿ Accessibility Enhancement - [x] 🔒 Security Update - [ ] 📦 Dependency Update - [ ] 🧹 Code Refactoring - [ ] 🧪 Test Updates --- ## 📷 Visual Changes No visual changes. This is a security-only update that preserves existing UI and animations. --- ## 🧪 Testing Performed ### 📱 Browser Compatibility - [x] Chrome (latest) - [ ] Firefox (Version: ) - [ ] Safari (Version: ) - [ ] Edge (Version: ) - [ ] Mobile Chrome (Device: ) - [ ] Mobile Safari (Device: ) ### 🖥️ Responsive Design - [x] Desktop (1200px+) - [x] Tablet (768px - 1199px) - [x] Mobile (320px - 767px) ### ✅ Test Cases 1. Verified that valid HTML and auto-linked URLs still render correctly. 2. Tested malicious HTML (e.g. ``) to confirm scripts are removed. 3. Confirmed no runtime errors or console warnings. --- ## ♿ Accessibility - [x] Proper heading hierarchy maintained - [x] ARIA labels added where needed - [x] Color contrast requirements met - [x] Keyboard navigation works correctly - [ ] Screen reader testing performed --- ## 📋 PR Checklist - [x] My code follows the project's coding style guidelines - [x] I have tested these changes locally - [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly - [x] My changes generate no new warnings or console errors - [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix/feature works - [x] All existing tests pass successfully - [x] I have checked for and resolved any merge conflicts - [ ] I have optimized images/assets (if applicable) - [ ] I have validated all links are working correctly --- ## 💭 Additional Notes This change sanitizes HTML content at the component boundary, ensuring the `FAQItem` component remains secure even if the data source changes in the future. The fix aligns with OWASP XSS prevention guidelines and React security best practices. --- ### 📚 Reviewer Resources - [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/sugarlabs/www-v2/blob/main/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md) - [Style Guide](https://github.com/sugarlabs/www-v2/blob/main/docs/dev_guide.md) - [Community Chat](https://matrix.to/#/#sugarlabs-web:matrix.org) Thank you for reviewing this security improvement! 🎉

prs.title: Added hover zoom effect to featured image in Principles section
prs.body: 🖼️Add Hover Zoom Effect to Featured Image in Principles Section 📌Problem Description:- The featured image in the Principles section was previously static and did not provide any visual interaction on hover. This made the section feel less engaging compared to other interactive elements on the page. After upgrading the UI to include a hover zoom effect, the implementation needed to ensure that: The zoom effect feels smooth and subtle. The image does not overflow its container. The gradient overlay and text remain visually intact and unaffected. ✅Solution Description:- This PR enhances the featured image by adding a hover zoom effect while maintaining layout stability and visual hierarchy. Key improvements:- Wrapped the image inside a container with overflow-hidden to prevent layout breakage. Applied the scale (zoom) effect only to the image using Framer Motion. Ensured the gradient overlay and text stay above the image using proper positioning and stacking. The zoom effect is subtle, smooth, and responsive across screen sizes. 🛠️Technical Details:- Framework: React Styling: Tailwind CSS Animation: Framer Motion Affected Section: Principles (featured image) 🎯Result:- Improved visual engagement on hover No overflow or overlap issues Clean, responsive, and accessible UI behavior Before hover:- After hover:- This PR addresses Issue #750

- [x] This pull request uses **HTML and CSS** only and provides a `meta.json` file.
- [x] All files in this pull request are in a directory named: `<github_username>-<art_name>` without the <>.
- [x] Your pull request contains `index.html` and `styles.css`.

- If your pull request meets the checklist requirements it will be approved and merged by a maintainer
- If your pull request needs some changes. A maintainer will leave a comment requesting changes
- If your pull request significantly violates the criteria in the checklist it will be closed with feedback.

issues.title: Avoid blocking alert() on clipboard copy failure in GlobalCard
issues.body:
Description

The copyURLToClipboard() function in planet/js/GlobalCard.js currently uses a blocking alert("Failed to copy!") when the ClipboardJS error callback is triggered. This interrupts the user workflow and freezes the UI, resulting in a poor user experience. The success case already provides non-blocking feedback via the console.

Current Behavior

When clipboard copy fails:

A blocking popup (alert) appears

The user cannot interact with the page until they click "OK"

The UI is temporarily frozen

Expected Behavior

When clipboard copy fails:

No blocking popup appears

A non-blocking message is logged (e.g., console.warn)

The UI remains fully responsive

Feedback is consistent with the success case

Proposed Approach (Optional)

Replace the alert("Failed to copy!") in the ClipboardJS error callback with:

console.warn("Failed to copy to clipboard:", e.action);


This keeps the failure feedback non-blocking and consistent with the success behavior.

No new UI elements are required for this minimal fix.

Environment

Operating System: All (Windows, macOS, Linux)

Browser (if applicable): All modern browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari)

Version of Software/Project: Latest master branch

Checklist
- [x] I have read and followed the project's code of conduct.
- [x] I have searched for similar issues before creating this one.
- [x] I have provided enough information to understand the issue.
- [x] I am willing to contribute to the resolution of this issue.
Thank you for contributing to our project! We appreciate your help in improving it.

📚 See [contributing instructions](https://github.com/sugarlabs/musicblocks/blob/master/README.md).

🙋🏾🙋🏼 Questions: [Community Matrix Server](https://matrix.to/#/#sugar:matrix.org).

issues.title: [Feature]: Added hover zoom effect to featured image in Principles section
issues.body: ### Feature Type UI/UX Enhancement ### Problem Description The Principles section’s featured image was static and lacked interactivity. Users could not engage with it visually, making the section feel less dynamic and modern. ### Proposed Solution This update introduces a hover zoom effect to the featured image in the Principles section. Now, when users hover over the static featured image, it slightly scales up, creating a more interactive and visually engaging experience. Changes Made: Wrapped the featured image inside a container with overflow-hidden to prevent overflow. Applied Framer Motion’s whileHover scale effect to the image. Gradient overlay and text remain above the image and are unaffected by the zoom. Before: Featured image was static and had no hover interaction. After: Featured image scales smoothly on hover without overflowing or breaking the overlay. Environment: Frontend framework: React + TailwindCSS + Framer Motion Repo/branch: www-v2 / main In short:- Added a hover zoom effect to the featured image using Framer Motion: The image slightly scales up on hover, creating an interactive experience. Wrapped the image in a container with overflow-hidden to prevent it from overflowing. The gradient overlay and text remain above the image, unaffected by the zoom. Smooth transition ensures the effect looks polished and responsive on all screen sizes. ### Alternative Solutions _No response_ ### Mockups or Examples _No response_ ### Implementation Details _No response_ ### Additional Context _No response_ ### Code of Conduct - [x] I have searched for similar feature requests before creating this one - [x] I understand this is a feature request for the website only, not Sugar Labs software - [x] I agree to follow the project's Code of Conduct
